title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: Status: Draft v0.1 Date: 2026-05-07 Target build model: Claude Opus 4.6 (claude-opus-4-6) Working name: prakriti (Sanskrit, “nature”). Fallback names taken CRAN: bharatpalettes, rangbhumi, IndiaPalettes.

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: prakriti R package provides curated color palettes inspired India’s natural landscapes - Himalaya, Thar, Kerala backwaters, Andaman reefs, Spiti cold desert, Kashmir’s chinar autumn, . mirrors API conventions established wesanderson, nationalparkcolors, MetBrewer, integrates first-class ggplot2, base graphics, scales.

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: Existing India-themed palette packages scarce; exist mostly Bollywood/textile-themed, landscape-themed. Palettes designed hand reference photography (auto-extracted), visually balanced named meaningfully. Coverage spans sequential, diverging, qualitative palette types - usable choropleths, heatmaps, categorical plots alike.

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: general-purpose color science library. CIELAB conversion utilities, perceptual uniformity guarantees beyond best-effort hand-tuning. Bollywood/cultural-motif palette pack. Scope strictly natural landscapes / biomes / geological features. auto-extract palettes user-supplied images.

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: 30 palettes total. palette stored named character vector hex codes R/palettes.R. Type metadata governs default ggplot2 scale behavior (continuous vs. discrete). Hex values listed §10 (locked spec).

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: name: palette name (string, must match names(prakriti_palettes)). n: number colors. NULL, returns full palette. type: "discrete" returns first n colors (recycled warning n > length); "continuous" interpolates via grDevices::colorRampPalette. direction: 1 -1 reverse. Returns: character vector hex codes, attr(., "type") attr(., "name") set.
code: prakriti_palette(name, n = NULL, type = c("discrete", "continuous"), direction = 1)

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: discrete = TRUE: wraps ggplot2::discrete_scale palette function. discrete = FALSE: wraps ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(colors = ...). Default discrete value depends palette type metadata supplied. Standard ggplot2 scale args (name, breaks, labels, na.value, guide) pass via ....
code: scale_color_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = TRUE, direction = 1) scale_colour_prakriti(...)   # alias scale_fill_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = TRUE, direction = 1)

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: display_prakriti(NULL) shows palettes stacked layout (one row per palette, similar RColorBrewer::display.brewer.).
code: prakriti_palettes        # named list, exported as data display_prakriti(name = NULL)   # plots one or all palettes (base R) prakriti_names()                # character vector of palette names prakriti_info(name)             # data.frame: name, type, n, inspiration

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
code: library(prakriti) library(ggplot2)  ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_prakriti("valley_of_flowers")  ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_raster() +   scale_fill_prakriti("himalaya", discrete = FALSE)  display_prakriti("rann")

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: tidyverse aggregate, Rcpp, system dependencies. Keep CRAN-friendly.

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: Coverage target: >= 90% measured via covr::package_coverage().

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: Every exported function: roxygen2 @param, @return, @examples, @export. Package-level docs prakriti-package.R _PACKAGE sentinel. palette appears ?prakriti_palettes inspiration string. README rendered README.Rmd includes hex-grid display 30 palettes (chunk: display_prakriti()). Vignette vignettes/prakriti.Rmd walks : choosing palette data type, ggplot2 examples (qualitative/sequential/diverging), choropleth example using sf + Indian state boundaries (data link, bundled).

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: Package name CRAN - confirm prakriti unclaimed via available::available("prakriti") scaffolding. Choropleth vignette - bundle small sf object Indian states, link geodata::gadm()? Bundling adds ~200KB; linking adds internet dependency vignette build. Lean: link. Color-blind safety - annotate palette deuteranopia/protanopia simulation results (colorspace::deutan)? Lean: yes sequential & diverging; qualitative palettes flagged cb_safe = FALSE applicable. Hindi/Sanskrit palette aliases - e.g., himalaya ↔︎ himalaya, thar ↔︎ marusthal. Lean: skip v1; revisit requested.

title: prakriti - Technical PRD
text: MIT License. Palettes original hand-tuned designs based reference photography public-domain Creative Commons sources; individual photo reproduced. CITATION file lists maintainer suggested BibTeX entry.

title: Getting started with prakriti
text: prakriti (Sanskrit nature) provides 30 hand-tuned color palettes inspired India’s natural landscapes. palette designed specific use case - sequential, diverging, qualitative - integrates directly ggplot2.
code: library(prakriti) library(ggplot2)

title: Getting started with prakriti
text: Get structured metadata prakriti_info():
code: prakriti_names() #>  [1] "himalaya"          "thar"              "backwaters"        #>  [4] "western_ghats"     "rann"              "valley_of_flowers" #>  [7] "andaman"           "nilgiri" title: Getting started with prakriti
text: Display single palette:  Display 30 palettes :
code: display_prakriti("valley_of_flowers") display_prakriti()

title: Getting started with prakriti
text: prakriti_palette() returns character vector hex codes: Reverse direction = -1: Interpolate number colors type = "continuous":
code: prakriti_palette("himalaya") #> [1] "#FCFEFF" "#A8D8EA" "#3D9BE9" "#1A5FB4" "#0D3B82" "#051B3E" #> attr(,"name") #> [1] "himalaya" #> attr(,"type") #> [1] "sequential" prakriti_palette("thar", n = 3) #> [1] "#FFF0A3" "#FFB727" "#F57D15" #> attr(,"name") #> [1] "thar" #> attr(,"type") #> [1] "sequential" prakriti_palette("chinar", direction = -1) #> [1] "#260000" "#7F0000" "#D50000" "#FF6F00" "#FFB300" "#FFECB3" #> attr(,"name") #> [1] "chinar" #> attr(,"type") #> [1] "sequential" prakriti_palette("nilgiri", n = 12, type = "continuous") #>  [1] "#E0F2E9" "#A4DEC7" "#68CAA6" "#46B08C" "#2C9574" "#1B8364" "#11755A" #>  [8] "#0A6650" "#075547" "#05433D" "#03332E" "#022420" #> attr(,"name") #> [1] "nilgiri" #> attr(,"type") #> [1] "sequential"

title: Getting started with prakriti
code: ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Petal.Length,                  color = Species, shape = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.85) +   scale_color_prakriti("valley_of_flowers") +   labs(title = "Iris dataset - valley_of_flowers palette") +   theme_minimal()

title: Getting started with prakriti
code: ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_raster(interpolate = TRUE) +   scale_fill_prakriti("himalaya") +   coord_cartesian(expand = FALSE) +   labs(title = "Old Faithful - himalaya palette") +   theme_minimal()

what: Diverging palettes (data with a meaningful midpoint)
title: Getting started with prakriti
code: cor_mat <- cor(mtcars[, 1:7]) cor_df  <- as.data.frame(as.table(cor_mat)) names(cor_df) <- c("var1", "var2", "rho")  ggplot(cor_df, aes(var1, var2, fill = rho)) +   geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.5) +   scale_fill_prakriti("mehrangarh", limits = c(-1, 1)) +   coord_equal() +   labs(title = "Correlation matrix - mehrangarh palette") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

title: Getting started with prakriti
text: default, qualitative palettes produce discrete scales sequential/diverging produce continuous scales. Override discrete:
code: # Force a sequential palette into discrete mode ggplot(mtcars, aes(factor(cyl), mpg, fill = factor(cyl))) +   geom_boxplot() +   scale_fill_prakriti("thar", discrete = TRUE) +   labs(title = "Forced discrete: thar palette") +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "none")

title: Getting started with prakriti
text: 24 detailed plot examples covering every palette type - stacked area, donut charts, calendar heatmaps, correlation tiles, dark-mode density fields, - see tutorial.R package root.

what: prakriti - Color Palettes from India's Natural Landscapes
title: prakriti - Color Palettes from India's Natural Landscapes
text: Color palettes inspired India’s natural landscapes. prakriti (Sanskrit, nature) R package providing 30 hand-tuned color palettes drawn India’s biomes geography - Himalayan snow, Thar dunes, Kerala backwaters, Andaman reefs, Spiti’s cold desert, Kashmir’s autumn chinar, . Palettes ship three flavors - sequential, diverging, qualitative - first-class ggplot2 integration.

title: prakriti - Color Palettes from India's Natural Landscapes
code: # remotes::install_github("orijitghosh/prakriti") devtools::load_all(".")  # during development

title: prakriti - Color Palettes from India's Natural Landscapes
code: library(prakriti) library(ggplot2)  # Discrete (qualitative) ggplot(iris, aes(Sepal.Length, Sepal.Width, color = Species)) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_prakriti("valley_of_flowers") +   theme_minimal()  # Continuous (sequential) ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_raster() +   scale_fill_prakriti("himalaya") +   theme_minimal()  # Inspect what's available prakriti_info() display_prakriti()

title: prakriti - Color Palettes from India's Natural Landscapes
text: See tutorial.R full visual walkthrough.
code: prakriti_palette(name, n = NULL, type = "discrete", direction = 1) scale_color_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = NULL, direction = 1) scale_fill_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = NULL, direction = 1) display_prakriti(name = NULL) prakriti_names() prakriti_info(name = NULL)

title: prakriti - Color Palettes from India's Natural Landscapes
text: MIT

what: Display one or all prakriti palettes — display_prakriti
title: Display one or all prakriti palettes — display_prakriti
text: Renders base-graphics swatch view one palette, stacked grid palettes. Useful quick visual inspection R console / RStudio Plots pane.

title: Display one or all prakriti palettes — display_prakriti
code: display_prakriti(name = NULL, show_type = TRUE)

title: Display one or all prakriti palettes — display_prakriti
text: name Character NULL. Name palette. NULL (default), displays 30 palettes stacked vertically. show_type Logical. TRUE (default), includes palette type (sequential, diverging, qualitative) row labels. Must TRUE FALSE - filter type, use prakriti_info() instead.

title: Display one or all prakriti palettes — display_prakriti
text: Invisibly returns NULL. Called side effect.

title: Display one or all prakriti palettes — display_prakriti
code: display_prakriti("rann")  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ display_prakriti() display_prakriti(show_type = FALSE) } # }

what: prakriti: Color palettes inspired by India's natural landscapes — prakriti-package
title: prakriti: Color palettes inspired by India's natural landscapes — prakriti-package
text: 30 hand-tuned color palettes drawn India's biomes geography - Himalayan snow, Thar dunes, Kerala backwaters, Andaman reefs, Spiti's cold desert, Kashmir's autumn chinar, . Includes ggplot2 scales, continuous interpolation, base-graphics display helpers.

title: prakriti: Color palettes inspired by India's natural landscapes — prakriti-package

what: Build a color palette from a prakriti palette — prakriti_palette
title: Build a color palette from a prakriti palette — prakriti_palette
text: Returns character vector hex codes drawn one named palettes prakriti_palettes. Supports discrete subsetting continuous interpolation via grDevices::colorRampPalette().

title: Build a color palette from a prakriti palette — prakriti_palette
code: prakriti_palette(   name,   n = NULL,   type = c("discrete", "continuous"),   direction = 1 )

title: Build a color palette from a prakriti palette — prakriti_palette
text: name Character. Name palette. See prakriti_names(). n Integer. Number colors return. NULL (default), returns full base palette unchanged. type Either "discrete" (subset / recycle base colors) "continuous" (interpolate smoothly across base colors). direction 1 (default) -1 reverse palette order.

title: Build a color palette from a prakriti palette — prakriti_palette
text: character vector hex codes attributes name type.

title: Build a color palette from a prakriti palette — prakriti_palette

title: Color palettes inspired by India's natural landscapes — prakriti_palettes
text: named list 30 palettes. element list : colors - character vector hex codes type - one "sequential", "diverging", "qualitative" inspiration - short prose description source landscape

what: ggplot2 color and fill scales for prakriti palettes — scale_prakriti
title: ggplot2 color and fill scales for prakriti palettes — scale_prakriti
text: functions plug prakriti palettes ggplot2. default scale type (discrete vs. continuous) chosen palette's metadata: qualitative palettes default discrete; sequential diverging palettes default continuous. Override discrete.

title: ggplot2 color and fill scales for prakriti palettes — scale_prakriti
code: scale_color_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = NULL, direction = 1)  scale_colour_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = NULL, direction = 1)  scale_fill_prakriti(name, ..., discrete = NULL, direction = 1)

title: ggplot2 color and fill scales for prakriti palettes — scale_prakriti
text: name Character. Name palette. See prakriti_names(). ... Additional arguments passed underlying ggplot2 scale constructor (ggplot2::discrete_scale(), ggplot2::scale_color_gradientn(), ggplot2::scale_fill_gradientn()). discrete Logical NULL. NULL (default), inferred palette's type. Set TRUE force discrete, FALSE continuous. direction 1 (default) -1 reverse palette order.

title: ggplot2 color and fill scales for prakriti palettes — scale_prakriti
text: ggplot2 scale object.

title: ggplot2 color and fill scales for prakriti palettes — scale_prakriti
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(ggplot2) ggplot(mtcars, aes(wt, mpg, color = factor(cyl))) +   geom_point(size = 3) +   scale_color_prakriti("valley_of_flowers")  ggplot(faithfuld, aes(waiting, eruptions, fill = density)) +   geom_raster() +   scale_fill_prakriti("himalaya") } # }

title: prakriti 0.1.0
text: Initial release. 30 palettes covering sequential, diverging, qualitative types, inspired India’s natural landscapes. prakriti_palette() raw hex vectors discrete continuous output. scale_color_prakriti() / scale_colour_prakriti() / scale_fill_prakriti() ggplot2. display_prakriti() base-graphics palette inspection. prakriti_names(), prakriti_info() discovery.
